steps.title: Read the sentence frame
steps.content: The main subject is the researchers, and the main verb is aimed. The words between commas must therefore form a modifier describing the researchers' method, not a second main verb.

steps.title: Use a participial modifier
steps.content: Choice A creates an awkward purpose phrase inside the comma pair. Choice C gives a finite past-tense verb, and Choice D gives a bare verb, both competing with aimed. A present participle phrase describes what the researchers were doing with computer imaging. The answer is B.
